reference_implementation.name: Project-folder ACLED MENA reference
reference_implementation.use_for_grading: Use as a canonical reference implementation for method shape, expected chart families, and qualitative findings. Do not require exact row counts because ACLED exports and the prompt's 2025-04-18 cutoff can differ from the repository snapshot.

meets_prompt: Uses ACLED MENA records across Middle East and Northern Africa.
Analyzes remote violence, air/drone strikes, border-proximate battles, and capital-area remote attacks.
Produces a self-contained HTML report with a research question, methods, findings, discussion-like interpretation, conclusion, and Plotly charts.
Includes an interactive geographic map with a yearly time slider.
caveats: Repository README describes the final analytical sample as 1997-2024, while the public task asks for 1997-01-01 to 2025-04-18.
The raw repository CSV inspected on 2026-04-25 contains records through 2025-04-19, but the final yearly/report summaries stop at 2024.
Therefore this reference should anchor methodology and expected qualitative direction, not an exact date-row-count match.

canonical_methods.remote_definition: event_type == 'Explosions/Remote violence'; air/drone strike is tracked via sub_event_type == 'Air/drone strike'
canonical_methods.capital_area_definition: event location is the national capital or begins with the capital name, e.g. 'Capital - ...'
canonical_methods.border_proximate_definition: event coordinate is within 50 km of an international land boundary using Natural Earth country boundaries
canonical_methods.overlap_handling: Capital-area and border-proximate events are not forced to be mutually exclusive; the reference reports 1,531 overlapping events in the 1997-2024 sample.

reference_key_findings: Remote violence and air/drone strikes rise sharply over the sample period.
The reference report does not find a clean spatial shift from border-proximate conflict toward capital-area targeting.
Capital-area remote events are a small share of remote violence; the reference cites 5,673 capital-area remote events, or 2.72% of remote events in the 1997-2024 sample.
Border-proximate remote events are much more common; the reference cites 116,845 border-proximate remote events, or 56.05% of remote events in the 1997-2024 sample.
Border-linked space remains a heavier conflict geography, even as remote attack methods expand.

map_requirements: Delivered HTML must contain an interactive geographic scatter/map using actual ACLED longitude/latitude coordinates.

The map must be geographically plausible for Middle East and Northern Africa records: no lat/lon swap, no mass of points at 0/0, no dominant point cloud outside the requested region, and no placeholder/random coordinates.
The map must expose temporal variation through a year/time slider, animation frame, or equivalent visible temporal control.
Capital-area and border-proximate status should be visible by color, facet, hover text, filter, or separate layers when practical.
chart_quality_requirements: Remote violence / air-drone trend and capital-vs-border composition charts must be based on event-level summaries, not hand-written placeholder numbers.
Chart titles, legends, axes, and hover labels should be readable enough to compare the output to references/project_folder_reference/docs/index.html.
The report should not earn a high score if the HTML is openable but the charts are blank, visually broken, or contradict the documented data transformations.

acceptable_variation: Executor may use a prompt-compliant ACLED export through 2025-04-18, so exact counts can differ from the 1997-2024 reference snapshot.
Executor may use equivalent ACLED current event-type labels if the export UI has changed.
A different substantive conclusion is acceptable only when backed by documented filters, reproducible variable construction, and visible charts from the downloaded data.
report_quality_anchors: capital vs border composition chart populated
interactive map points in MENA with time variation
method and findings broadly align with the reference implementation
